title: dubautia carrii
intro: Dubautia carrii, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Hawaii. It is a type of flowering plant that has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as offering protection against certain diseases. Dubautia carrii is typically prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, or salves, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
